rationale: Adds two new GitHub Actions workflows that match the PR description exactly. `.github/workflows/link-check.yml` (102 lines) installs lychee v0.18.0, scans `**/*.md` excluding `.git`, `artefactos`, `node_modules`, `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `example.com`, `tu-.*\.com`, with `--accept '200..=299,403,429'`, then uses `actions/github-script@v7` to create or update an issue labeled `links-rotos` only on scheduled runs when `EXIT_CODE != 0` and the report file is non-empty. `.github/workflows/release.yml` (81 lines) triggers on `v*` tag pushes, extracts version from the tag, uses awk to pull the matching `## [VERSION]` section from `CHANGELOG.md` (with a sane fallback message), sets up Python 3.12, runs `python validar_repo.py --strict` as a gate, then publishes via `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` with `prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.tag, '-') }}`. Both workflows do what the body promises; no cheat or stub.
